expectations.text: uses_impact: Uses the IMPACT() function (not BISTOP or a manual IF expression)
expectations.evidence: IMPACT(...) is used explicitly in both the standalone expression block and the force_vector command.

expectations.text: correct_displacement_arg: First arg uses DZ or similar displacement measuring penetration below floor
expectations.evidence: First argument is DZ(.model.body.cm, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref), measuring Z-displacement of CM relative to the floor reference marker.

expectations.text: correct_velocity_arg: Second arg uses VZ or matching velocity measure
expectations.evidence: Second argument is VZ(.model.body.cm, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref, .model.ground.floor_ref), matching the DZ reference frame.

expectations.text: threshold_at_zero: Threshold value is 0.0 (or equivalent contact point)
expectations.evidence: Third argument (trigger/threshold) is 0.0; table confirms 'Contact activates when DZ < 0'.

expectations.text: correct_params: Stiffness 1e5, exponent 1.5, damping 50, penetration 0.1 all present
expectations.evidence: Parameters are 1.0E5, 1.5, 50.0, 0.1 — all four correct values present in the IMPACT call.

expectations.text: quoted_expression: Expression wrapped in double quotes as required for FUNCTION= parameter
expectations.evidence: In the force_vector command, function_z segments are all wrapped in double quotes; function_x and function_y use "0.0".
